science.title: These Tiny Robots 50x Smaller Than a Hair Can Hunt and Move Bacteria
science.description: Photon-driven nanorobots can steer, capture, and move bacteria with precision, enabling controlled manipulation in microscopic environments and offering new tools for microbiology.

science.title: DNA research just rewrote the origin of human species
science.description: Scientists have uncovered a surprising new picture of human origins that challenges the long-held idea of a single ancestral population in Africa. By analyzing genetic data from diverse modern African groups—especially the highly distinct Nama people—and comparing it with fossil evidence, researchers found that early humans likely evolved from multiple intermingling populations over hundreds of thousands of years. Rather than a clean split, these groups stayed connected, exchanging genes even after beginning to diverge around 120,000–135,000 years ago.

science.title: Physicist Proposes Dark Matter Is Made of Black Holes That Survived Dead Universes
science.description: Dark matter could be made of "relic" black holes that survived the collapse of previous universes, a new model proposes.

ai.title: Indian IT Giants Embrace AI Amidst Macroeconomic Shifts
ai.description: India's leading IT firms, TCS, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro, and Tech Mahindra, face structural changes driven by AI and macroeconomic factors. While traditional services report deflation, AI innovations fuel new revenue streams. Companies remain optimistic as they navigate geopolitical challenges and plan for a future defined by AI-led growth.

ai.title: AI-Driven E-Checkposts Revolutionize Mining Oversight in Singrauli
ai.description: In Singrauli, Madhya Pradesh, AI-powered e-checkposts have been installed to combat illegal mineral transportation, improve revenue, and enhance transparency. The initiative, featuring advanced surveillance technology, aims to reinforce regulations around mining activities in the district, known as India's 'Energy Capital' due to its coal resources.

ai.title: Threat from Anthropic’s Mythos 'as big as war': Nirmala Sitharaman
ai.description: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has warned of a new cyber threat from Anthropic's AI model Mythos, comparing its potential impact to that of war. Authorities are increasing engagement with Anthropic and the US administration to understand and counter these evolving digital risks, while also strengthening domestic capabilities and financial sector preparedness.
